_meta.purpose: JSON-LD robustness test fixtures for COTSK-9 (STEAL 6).
_meta.shape: Each case is { name, description, html, expected }. `html` is a minimal HTML fragment exercising one edge case. `expected` is the JobPosting fields the parser should extract (or null if the case should fail gracefully).
_meta.casing: title used instead of full job_posting since extractJsonLdBlocks + findJobPosting + parseJobPosting are the full pipeline under test

cases.name: 01-strict-clean-jobposting
cases.description: Baseline: strict-clean JSON-LD JobPosting. Must continue to work after refactor.

cases.name: 02-html-comment-wrap
cases.description: Trick 1: JSON-LD wrapped in <!-- ... --> inside the script tag.

cases.name: 03-graph-array-jobposting
cases.description: Trick 2: JobPosting nested under @graph array alongside WebPage.

cases.name: 04-top-level-array
cases.description: Trick 2 edge: top-level array of nodes (no @graph wrapper). Schema.org allows this.

cases.name: 05-trailing-commas
cases.description: Trick 3: JSON with trailing commas before } and ]. Strict JSON.parse rejects; lenient should recover.

cases.name: 06-inline-block-comment
cases.description: Trick 3: JSON with inline /* */ comment. Strict JSON.parse rejects; lenient should recover.
cases.html: <html><head><script type="application/ld+json">{"@type":"JobPosting","title":"Block Comment Role" /* injected by build tool */, "jobLocationType":"HYBRID"}</script></head></html>

cases.name: 07-unrecoverable-malformed
cases.description: Trick 3 floor: garbage that neither strict nor lenient can parse. Must fail gracefully (return null) and log a WARN — not throw.
cases.html: <html><head><script type="application/ld+json">{ this is not json at all :: }}}</script></head></html>
